'''Joseph Hayes''' (born 4 January 1976) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. He played at representative level for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]], and at club level for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]], [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] and [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]], as a {{rlp|wg}}.

==Club career==
Hayes played {{rlp|rw}} and scored a [[Try (rugby)|try]] in [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]]' 16–25 defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1995–96 League Cup (rugby league)|1995–96 Regal Trophy]] Final during the [[1995–96 Rugby Football League season|1995–96]] at [[John Smith's Stadium|Alfred McAlpine Stadium]], [[Huddersfield]] on Saturday 13 January 1996.<ref name="13th January 1996: St Helens 16 Wigan 25 (Regal Trophy Final)">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2976|title=13th January 1996: St Helens 16 Wigan 25 (Regal Trophy Final)|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2014|access-date=1 January 2015|archive-date=7 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181007005311/http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2976|url-status=dead}}</ref>

After retiring as a player, Hayes re-joined St Helens as a physiotherapist, a role which he held until 2014.<ref>{{cite web|title=Joey Hayes moves on from Saints|url=http://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/sport/11584581.Joey_Hayes_moves_on_from_Saints|website=St Helens Star|publisher=Newsquest|access-date=18 July 2017|date=6 November 2014}}</ref>

==International honours==
Joey Hayes won a [[Cap (sport)|cap]] for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] while at St. Helens in 1996 against Papua New Guinea.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>
